response: {"purpose":"Figure 2b demonstrates that normalizing eCLIP clusters against a paired Size-Matched Input (SMInput) substantially improves the ability to rank and identify biologically meaningful binding sites over pre-normalized (CLIPper) p-values alone. By selecting 2,821 SLBP eCLIP clusters significant under either scoring method and comparing their rankings side by side, the panel shows that SMInput normalization preferentially elevates histone RNA-overlapping clusters—the known, expected targets of SLBP—toward the top of the ranked list. The shift in rank for the 271 histone-overlapping clusters is quantified using a Kolmogorov-Smirnov test, providing statistical evidence that normalization meaningfully reorders clusters rather than merely rescaling them. This matters because it establishes SMInput normalization as a principled strategy for reducing background noise and improving the signal-to-noise ratio in eCLIP data, thereby enabling more confident identification of true protein–RNA interactions.","title":"SMInput Normalization Improves Ranking of True SLBP Binding Sites"}
